subject: Comments on your Chapter 3 draft
snippet: Alex - I read through the methods section last night and overall it's...
body:
Alex,

I read through the methods section last night and overall it's shaping up well. A few things before our Friday check-in:

1. The tide-window justification needs a citation - see Menge & Sutherland for the disturbance framing.
2. Table 2 mixes site codes and site names; pick one and stay consistent.
3. The warming-anomaly figure is strong, but add the 2025 baseline as a dashed line so the comparison reads at a glance.

No need to rush a full rewrite - focus on the methods and we'll talk results Friday. Nice progress.

Best,
Sarah

subject: Re: Comments on your Chapter 3 draft
snippet: Thank you - this is really helpful. I'll pull the Menge & Sutherland...
body:
Dr. Lindström,

Thank you - this is really helpful. I'll pull the Menge & Sutherland citation and clean up the site naming today. The dashed 2025 baseline is a good call; I have that series in the Airtable export so it should be quick.

I'll bring a revised methods section and a first pass at the results narrative to Friday's check-in.

Thanks again,
Alex

subject: Re: Comments on your Chapter 3 draft
snippet: One more thing - let's aim to have the full chapter draft to me by...
body:
Alex,

One more thing - let's aim to have the full chapter draft to me by Oct 16 so the committee has time before the December seminar. EOD that Friday is fine; I'll read over the weekend.

Also, Marcus Hull asked to see the warming-anomaly analysis when it's ready - he may have chemistry context that helps frame the discussion.

See you Friday at 2.

Sarah

subject: Re: Comments on your Chapter 3 draft
snippet: Sounds good - Oct 16 EOD works. I'll loop in Dr. Hull once the...
body:
Dr. Lindström,

Sounds good - Oct 16 EOD works. I'll have the full chapter to you that Friday. I'll loop in Dr. Hull on the warming-anomaly analysis once the figures are finalized so he's looking at the clean version.

See you Friday.

Alex
